meta.certificationName: Microsoft Certified: Fabric Data Engineer Associate (DP-700)
meta.title: MS-FDE-6: OneLake Virtualization, Spark Delivery, and Real-Time Operations

questions.stem: What is a core benefit of a Fabric lakehouse?

options.text: It combines data-lake scalability with SQL and Spark access over Delta data in OneLake
options.explanation: The lakehouse overview emphasizes OneLake storage, Delta format, and access from both Spark and SQL experiences.

options.text: It requires copying data into a separate BI-only engine for every query

questions.stem: A Fabric pipeline should copy files from ADLS Gen2 into a lakehouse table on a schedule. Which Fabric capability is designed for this?

options.text: Data Factory pipeline with copy activity
options.explanation: Fabric pipelines and copy activity are designed for orchestrated data movement into Fabric destinations.

questions.reference: [Copy activity in data pipelines for Data Factory in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-factory/copy-data-activity)

questions.stem: A data engineer wants to transform large Delta tables with PySpark in Fabric. Which item is the BEST fit?

options.explanation: Fabric notebooks are the native environment for Spark-based data engineering with languages such as PySpark.

questions.stem: A team needs to ingest and analyze streaming telemetry in Fabric with low-latency experiences. Which Fabric area is MOST relevant?

options.explanation: Real-Time Intelligence is Fabric's area for streaming/event-centric analytics experiences.

questions.stem: Which statement BEST describes monitoring in Fabric operations?

options.text: Engineers should review run history and activity signals for pipelines and items to detect failures and usage patterns
options.explanation: Operational monitoring in Fabric includes activity and usage visibility so teams can detect failures and capacity or usage issues.

options.text: Fabric monitoring is unnecessary because managed services never fail
Only Power BI visuals can be monitored
Monitoring is limited to local notebook logs

questions.stem: A lakehouse table suffers from many small files after repeated incremental loads. Which maintenance pattern is commonly used?

options.text: Optimize file layout and clean obsolete files in Delta tables
options.explanation: Small-file issues are typically addressed with Delta maintenance patterns such as compaction-oriented optimization and cleanup.

questions.stem: A project needs a secure collaboration boundary for permissions over Fabric items. Which concept is foundational?

options.explanation: Workspaces are a core administrative and permission boundary in Fabric.

questions.stem: A team wants to query operational telemetry in a KQL-style experience inside Fabric. Which language should they expect to use?

options.explanation: The certification overview explicitly notes that Fabric data engineers should be skilled in KQL alongside SQL and PySpark.

questions.stem: A department wants to expose data already stored in another system through OneLake without copying it into a second physical location. Which feature is BEST?

options.explanation: OneLake shortcuts provide live access to existing data locations without creating another full copy.

questions.stem: A downstream notebook should run only if an upstream copy activity succeeds. Which pipeline concept enables this?

options.explanation: Pipeline activities can be sequenced with success/failure dependencies so downstream steps run only when prerequisites succeed.

questions.stem: A Fabric engineer chooses a lakehouse instead of a warehouse because the workload includes unstructured files and Spark-first engineering. Why does that choice fit?

options.text: Lakehouses are built for Spark-oriented engineering over structured and unstructured data
options.explanation: The lakehouse overview contrasts lakehouses with warehouses by highlighting Spark-first development and broader data-type flexibility.

options.text: Warehouses are the only place Delta can exist

questions.stem: An operations team wants a central place to discover and bring streaming data into Fabric experiences. Which capability helps MOST directly?

options.explanation: Real-Time hub is the Fabric entry point for discovering and bringing streaming/event data into real-time solutions.

questions.stem: A pipeline fails intermittently after upstream source throttling. What is the BEST first operational response?

options.text: Inspect pipeline run history and retry/error details before changing architecture
options.explanation: Run history and activity details are the right first place to investigate transient pipeline failures in a managed platform.

options.text: Convert the source to a Power BI visual

questions.stem: A Fabric SQL endpoint scans far more data than expected because the table stores many historical partitions that analysts rarely need. Which design change may help MOST?

options.text: Organize data and retention so common queries touch less data
options.explanation: Performance usually improves when storage layout and retention align with query patterns so engines read less unnecessary data.

options.text: Move the table name to uppercase

questions.stem: A team shares a workspace with contractors but wants to limit access to only the necessary Fabric items. What principle should guide the design?

options.text: Least privilege through workspace and item permissions
options.explanation: Least privilege is the core governance approach for controlling access within collaborative workspaces.

options.text: Grant everyone Admin so work is faster
Hide item names instead of controlling access

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about OneLake shortcuts are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: They can reduce unnecessary data copies
options.explanation: Shortcuts are virtual pointers that help avoid extra copies, but they can break if the target path changes. Deleting the shortcut does not delete the target.

options.text: If the target path changes or is removed, the shortcut can break
options.explanation: Shortcuts are virtual pointers that help avoid extra copies, but they can break if the target path changes. Deleting the shortcut does not delete the target.

options.text: Deleting a shortcut always deletes the target data
Shortcuts can be created only in local desktop folders

questions.stem: A data movement process needs parameterized reusable pipelines for multiple source systems. Which Fabric capability is MOST useful?

options.explanation: Fabric pipelines support reusable orchestration patterns with configurable activities and parameters.

questions.stem: A team needs collaborative engineering work that mixes markdown explanation, SQL, and PySpark in one asset. What is the BEST fit?

options.explanation: Notebooks are designed for code plus narrative in a single collaborative engineering artifact.

questions.stem: Which scenario BEST fits Real-Time Intelligence instead of a batch-only lakehouse load?

options.text: Second-by-second sensor telemetry analysis and alert-oriented exploration
options.explanation: Real-Time Intelligence is intended for low-latency event and telemetry use cases rather than infrequent batch-only ingestion.

questions.stem: A platform owner wants to understand which items are heavily used and which are failing most often. What should they review?

options.explanation: Usage and activity monitoring help identify hot spots, failures, and adoption trends across Fabric items.

questions.stem: A team says a Fabric lakehouse and Fabric warehouse are interchangeable in every respect. Why is that inaccurate?

options.text: They share storage fundamentals but differ in primary development patterns, supported scenarios, and transactional characteristics
options.explanation: The docs distinguish lakehouses and warehouses by tooling, data-type emphasis, and workload patterns even though both use OneLake and Delta-based foundations.

options.text: They are exactly the same product with different icons

questions.stem: A pipeline copies raw files successfully, but downstream reporting shows duplicate rows after reruns. What design improvement is MOST important?

options.text: Make the load pattern idempotent or add deduplication logic rather than assuming retries are harmless
options.explanation: Reliable data delivery needs rerun-safe ingestion patterns; retries without idempotency can create duplicates.

questions.reference: [Copy activity in data pipelines for Data Factory in Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-factory/copy-data-activity)

questions.stem: A Spark notebook works in development but fails in production because dependent data paths differ between workspaces. What is the BEST engineering lesson?

options.text: Externalize environment-specific configuration instead of hard-coding workspace-specific paths
options.explanation: Promotion-friendly engineering avoids hard-coded environment details and uses configurable paths or parameters.

options.text: PySpark should be replaced with PowerPoint

questions.stem: A team wants dashboards backed by fresh event data with minimal copy latency. Which statement is MOST accurate?

options.text: Real-Time Intelligence and related ingestion experiences are intended for low-latency event workflows
options.explanation: Fabric's real-time stack exists specifically for event-driven, low-latency analytics scenarios.

options.text: Only nightly batch pipelines can feed dashboards in Fabric
OneLake shortcuts are the only streaming mechanism
KQL cannot be used in real-time scenarios

questions.stem: A managed Fabric solution still misses SLAs because engineers discover failures hours later. What should change FIRST?

options.text: Establish active operational monitoring and response processes instead of assuming managed services remove the need for observability
options.explanation: Managed platforms reduce infrastructure burden, but teams still need monitoring and response disciplines to meet SLAs.

questions.stem: An engineer repeatedly reloads the same large historical dataset into a second lakehouse for convenience. Which alternative may be better?

options.text: Use OneLake shortcuts when live access without duplication satisfies the use case
options.explanation: Shortcuts are specifically intended to reduce unnecessary data copies across domains, clouds, and accounts.

options.text: Create a third copy as backup for every analyst

questions.stem: A workspace contains notebooks, pipelines, and semantic assets for multiple teams with different responsibilities. What is the BEST governance approach?

options.text: Use workspace/item roles carefully so contributors get only the permissions they need
options.explanation: Role-based access and least privilege are the appropriate governance controls for shared Fabric workspaces.

options.text: Grant Admin to all users to reduce ticket volume
Rely on hidden file names for security

questions.stem: A Fabric engineer needs to explore streaming operational data with KQL syntax. Which workload path is the strongest match?

options.text: Use Fabric's real-time/KQL experiences rather than forcing the scenario into a batch-only SQL pattern
options.explanation: The certification scope includes KQL skills, and Fabric's real-time experiences are the natural place for that language and workload style.

options.text: Avoid KQL because the certification never mentions it
Convert events to static PDFs first

questions.stem: A shortcut points to an external path. The source team renames the target folder. What is the MOST likely result?

options.text: The shortcut can break until the target path is corrected
options.explanation: The OneLake shortcuts documentation notes that if the target path is moved, renamed, or deleted, the shortcut can break.

options.text: The shortcut automatically migrates the target with no dependency

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about Fabric pipelines are correct? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Fabric pipelines are used for orchestration and scheduled workflow coordination. The other options do not describe their purpose.

options.text: They are useful for scheduled data movement and workflow coordination
options.explanation: Fabric pipelines are used for orchestration and scheduled workflow coordination. The other options do not describe their purpose.

options.text: They are only for dashboard color changes

questions.stem: A team wants a single place for code, markdown explanation, and Spark execution. Which item provides that?

options.explanation: Notebooks combine executable Spark code and documentation in one place.

questions.stem: A solution analyzes manufacturing signals as they arrive and routes operators to current anomalies. Which Fabric area belongs in the design?

options.explanation: That scenario is real-time event analytics, which fits Fabric's Real-Time Intelligence capabilities.

questions.stem: Which action helps an engineer verify whether a scheduled pipeline failed because of an upstream source issue?

options.text: Review execution history and error details for the failed run
options.explanation: Operational diagnosis starts with run history and failure details.

questions.stem: Analysts query recent data far more often than old history. Which broad optimization idea is BEST?

options.text: Align storage layout and maintenance with the common access pattern so engines scan less data
options.explanation: Performance tuning starts with aligning layout and maintenance to real query behavior.

questions.stem: A Fabric engineer is asked to give an external partner temporary access to one project area only. Which boundary should they start from?

options.text: The relevant workspace and its roles
options.explanation: Workspaces are the core collaboration and permission boundary to start from.

questions.stem: Which statement about a Fabric lakehouse is TRUE?

options.text: It supports both Spark-based engineering and SQL-based analysis over Delta data in OneLake
options.explanation: The lakehouse overview emphasizes shared OneLake storage plus both Spark and SQL access patterns.

options.text: It can store only structured warehouse tables
It requires separate storage for analysts

questions.stem: A recurring ingestion job should be easy to rerun, schedule, and chain with downstream steps. What is the BEST implementation anchor?

options.explanation: Pipelines are the orchestration anchor for repeatable, schedulable, chained ingestion processes.

questions.stem: A team prefers Python-based transformation logic over SQL-first warehouse development. Which Fabric storage/compute pattern aligns best?

options.explanation: Spark notebooks over a lakehouse are the natural fit for Python-oriented data engineering.

questions.stem: A managed platform still requires alerting because:

options.text: Managed services reduce infrastructure work but do not eliminate job failures, source issues, or SLA obligations
options.explanation: Managed does not mean failure-free; teams still need observability to uphold data-product commitments.

options.text: Monitoring is only for on-premises systems
Users never care about failed refreshes

questions.stem: A department copies the same source data into several lakehouses just so different teams can see it. Which redesign should they evaluate first?

options.text: Use OneLake shortcuts where possible to reduce duplicate copies while keeping access virtualized
options.explanation: OneLake shortcuts are designed specifically to unify access and reduce unnecessary duplication.

options.text: Create even more copies to improve governance
